title: Migration Project
description: Shape of project.yaml — the long-lived per-engagement config: client meta, the sandbox load target, per-source-table source_key declarations (for identity), and the list of snapshots/drops. See 01-operating-model.md §1, §2.

slug.description: URL/dir-safe project identifier; also the project directory name.

legacy_system.description: Name/vendor of the source system the data was exported from.

target.description: The Everspot sandbox tenant this project loads into. Wave-0 introspection and the eventual load point here.

token_env_var.description: Name of the env var that holds the Orion API token (the token itself is never committed).

user_id_header.description: Value for the Orion `user-id` header — the migration user (must hold the relevant Spatie viewAny/create/update permissions). See plan §2.1.

tenant_id.description: Stancl tenant UUID, if known. Optional; informational.

ip_whitelisted.description: Confirmation that the pipeline's egress IP is whitelisted on the sandbox tenant.

sources.description: One entry per source table. The source_key declaration drives source_id computation (01-operating-model.md §2). If a table's key is unknown it is deferred to a Gate-1 question rather than guessed.

description.description: What this table holds in the client's system.

source_key.description: Column(s) that uniquely identify a record in the client's system. Composite keys are ordered. Omit/empty only when key is unknown — then set key_status:deferred.

key_status.description: confirmed = declared key trusted; deferred = unknown, raise a Gate-1 question; hashed = no stable key, fall back to deterministic hash of identifying columns (flag fragility).

key_columns.description: When key_status=hashed, the identifying columns hashed to derive source_key.

snapshots.description: The drops received for this project, in order. Raw bytes live under snapshots/<id>/raw/ and are immutable.
